passing_score_scale: 9–999 scale; passing varies by level (400 for Levels 1 and 2-CE, 350 for Level 3)
exam_format: Linear computer-based (Levels 1, 2-CE, 3 at Prometric); OSCE (Level 2-PE, suspended)

testing_centers.notes: COMLEX-USA Levels 1, 2-CE, and 3 administered at Prometric testing centers. Schedule through the NBOME MyNBOME candidate portal at nbome.org.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match NBOME registration exactly.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, or reference materials permitted at Prometric centers. COMLEX-USA Level 2-PE (OSCE) is currently suspended as of 2026. Results typically available within 3–6 weeks of examination.
renewal_options: COMLEX-USA scores do not expire. DO licensure itself is subject to state board continuing medical education (CME) requirements, which vary by state.

prerequisites.description: COMLEX-USA Level 1 and Level 2-CE passage; taken during or after residency training

retake_policy.notes: 60-day waiting period between failed attempts. Maximum 4 attempts per COMLEX-USA level. Candidates who exhaust attempts must petition NBOME for additional eligibility.

official_study_resources.title: COMLEX-USA Bulletin of Information
official_study_resources.notes: Official bulletin with content specifications, eligibility requirements, and policies.
